description.div: Since the end of WWII, Penghu is administered by the Republic of China and is its smalles county. Through the Cairo Declaration, the Sino-American Mutual Defense Treaty and the subsequent Taiwan Relations Act, the United States have recgonized Penghu as part of the Republic of China. Penghu County is represented since 2008 in the Legislative Yuan of the Republic of China by one at-large single-member constituency, the Penghu County Constituency (澎湖縣選舉區, Pénghú Xiàn Xuǎnjǔ Qū). The Penghu County Council (澎湖縣議會, Pēnghú Xiàn Yìhuì) is the elected county council of Penghu County. The council has 19 members and has, since it last election on 26 November 2022, one councilor of the Non-Partisan Solidarity Union, three DPP councilors, four KMT councilors and 11 independent, non-affiliated councilors.
Chidong Village, a village on Xiyu, Penghu.

names.name: South Penghu Marine National Park
澎湖南方四島國家公園
澎湖縣政府工程成果牌

Niaoyu Branch Fire Bureau, Penghu County

description.div: The name Penghu refers to the Archipelago, but also to the main island of this archipelago. The main island is where the captial Magong, Xiyu District and Pengnan, administered by Magong City are located.

names.name: Magong Wen Ao Wu Ying North

ent.description: The Xingren Village Community Monument is just beside the north general, indicating that both are markers of community boundaries. While the general system has an apotropaic function, this monument focuses more on local identity and maybe tourism.
